usageSummary: The ipv6 prefix-list command is similar to the ip prefix-list command, except that it is IPv6-specific.  To suppress networks from being advertised in updates, use the distribute-list out command.  The sequence number of a prefix list entry determines the order of the entries in the list.

name: ipv6 source-guard attach-policy
detail: Apply IPv6 source guard policy on an interface.
usageSummary: If no policy is specified using the source-guard-policy argument, then the default source-guard policy is applied.  A dependency exists between IPv6 source guard and IPv6 snooping.  Whenever IPv6 source guard is configured, when the ipv6 source-guard attach-policy command is entered, it verifies that snooping is enabled and issues a warning if it is not.

name: ipv6 source-route
detail: Enable processing of the IPv6 type 0 routing header (the IPv6 source routing header).
usageSummary: The default was changed to be the no version of the ipv6 source-route command, which means this functionality is not enabled.  Before this change, this functionality was enabled automatically.  User who had configured the no ipv6 source-route command before the default was changed will continue to see this configuration in their show config command output, even though the no version of the command is the default.

name: ipv6 spd mode
detail: Configure an IPv6 Selective Packet Discard (SPD) mode.
syntax: ipv6 spd mode {aggressive | tos protocol ospf}
noForm: no ipv6 spd mode {aggressive | tos protocol ospf}

params.name: aggressive
params.description: Aggressive drop mode discards incorrectly formatted packets when the IPv6 SPD is in random drop state.

params.name: tos protocol o spf
params.description: OSPF mode allows OSPF packets to be handled with SPD priority.

usageSummary: The default setting for the IPv6 SPD mode is none, but you may want to use the ipv6 spd mode command to configure a mode to be used when a certain SPD state is reached.  The aggressive keyword enables aggressive drop mode, which drops deformed packets when IPv6 SPD is in random drop state.  The ospf keyword enables OSPF mode, in which OSPF packets are handled with SPD priority.

name: ipv6 spd queue max-threshold
detail: Configure the maximum number of packets in the IPv6 Selective Packet Discard (SPD) process input queue.

params.name: value
params.description: Number of packets. The range is from 0 through 65535.

usageSummary: Use the ipv6 spd queue max-threshold command to configure the SPD queue maximum threshold value.  The size of the process input queue governs the SPD state: normal (no drop), random drop, or max.  When the process input queue is less than the SPD minimum threshold, SPD takes no action and enters normal state.

name: ipv6 traffic interface-statistics
detail: Collect IPv6 forwarding statistics for all interfaces.

params.name: unclearable
params.description: (Optional) IPv6 forwarding statistics are kept for all interfaces, but it is not possible to clear the statistics on any interface.

usageSummary: Using the optional unclearable keyword halves the per-interface statistics storage requirements.

name: ipv6 unicast-routing
detail: Enable the forwarding of IPv6 unicast datagrams.
usageSummary: Configuring the no ipv6 unicast-routing command removes all IPv6 routing protocol entries from the IPv6 routing table.

name: key chain
detail: Define an authentication key chain needed to enable authentication for routing protocols and enter key-chain configuration mode.

params.name: name-of-chain
params.description: Name of a key chain. A key chain must have at least one key and can have up to 2147483647 keys.

modes: Usage Guidelines You must configure a key chain with keys to enable authentication. Although you can identify multiple key chains, we recommend using one key chain per interface per routing protocol. Upon specifying the key chain command, you enter key chain configuration mode.
Examples The following example shows how to specify key chain:
accept-lifetime Sets the time period during which the authentication key on a key chain is received as valid.
key Identifies an authentication key on a key chain.
key-string (authentication) Specifies the authentication string for a key.
send-lifetime Sets the time period during which an authentication key on a key chain is valid to be sent.
show key chain Displays authentication key information.
Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 636 IP Addressing Services key-string (authentication)
usageSummary: You must configure a key chain with keys to enable authentication.  Although you can identify multiple key chains, we recommend using one key chain per interface per routing protocol.  Upon specifying the key chain command, you enter key chain configuration mode.

name: key-string
detail: Specify the authentication string for a key.

params.name: text
params.description: Authentication string that must be sent and received in the packets using the routing protocol being authenticated. The string can contain from 1 to 80 uppercase and lowercase alphanumeric characters.

modes: Examples The following example shows how to specify the authentication string for a key:
accept-lifetime Sets the time period during which the authentication key on a key chain is received as valid.
key Identifies an authentication key on a key chain.
key chain Defines an authentication key-chain needed to enable authentication for routing protocols.
send-lifetime Sets the time period during which an authentication key on a key chain is valid to be sent.
show key chain Displays authentication key information.
Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 637 IP Addressing Services

name: key
detail: Identify an authentication key on a key chain.

params.name: key-id
params.description: Identification number of an authentication key on a key chain. The range of keys is from 0 to 2147483647. The key identification numbers need not be consecutive.

modes: Usage Guidelines It is useful to have multiple keys on a key chain so that the software can sequence through the keys as they become invalid after time, based on the accept-lifetime and send-lifetime key chain key command settings. Each key has its own key identifier, which is stored locally. The combination of the key identifier and the interface associated with the message uniquely identifies the authentication algorithm and Message Digest 5 (MD5) authentication key in use. Only one authentication packet is sent, regardless of the number of valid keys. The software starts looking at the lowest key identifier number and uses the first valid key. If the last key expires, authentication will continue and an error message will be generated. To disable authentication, you must manually delete the last valid key. To remove all keys, remove the key chain by using the no key chain command.
Examples The following example shows how to specify a key to identify authentication on a key-chain:
accept-lifetime Sets the time period during which the authentication key on a key chain is received as valid.
key chain Defines an authentication key chain needed to enable authentication for routing protocols.
key-string (authentication) Specifies the authentication string for a key.
send-lifetime Sets the time period during which an authentication key on a key chain is valid to be sent.
show key chain Displays authentication key information.
Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 638 IP Addressing Services nat64 enable
usageSummary: It is useful to have multiple keys on a key chain so that the software can sequence through the keys as they become invalid after time, based on the accept-lifetime and send-lifetime key chain key command settings.  Each key has its own key identifier, which is stored locally.  The combination of the key identifier and the interface associated with the message uniquely identifies the authentication algorithm and Message Digest 5 (MD5) authentication key in use.

name: nat64 enable
detail: Enable Network Address Translation 64 (NAT64) on an interface.

name: nat64 v6v4
detail: Translate an IPv6 source address to an IPv4 source address and an IPv4 destination address to an IPv6 destination address for Network Address Translation 64 (NAT64).
syntax: nat64 v6v4 {list access-list-name pool pool-name [overload] | static {ipv6-address ipv4-address | tcp ipv6-address port ipv4-address port | udp ipv6-address port ipv4-address port}} tcp ipv6-address port ipv4-address port | udp ipv6-address port ipv4-address port}} [forced]
noForm: no nat64 v6v4 {list access-list-name pool pool-name [overload] | static {ipv6-address ipv4-address |

params.name: list
params.description: Associates an IPv4 pool with the filtering mechanism that decides when to apply an IPv6 address mapping.

params.name: access-list-name
params.description: Name of the IPv6 access list.

params.name: pool
params.description: Specifies the NAT64 pool for dynamic mapping of addresses.

params.name: pool-name
params.description: Name of the NAT64 pool.

params.name: overload
params.description: (Optional) Enables NAT64 overload address translation.

params.name: static
params.description: Enables NAT64 static mapping of addresses.

params.name: ipv6-address
params.description: IPv6 address of the IPv6 host to which static mapping is applied.

params.name: ipv4-address
params.description: IPv4 address that represents the IPv6 host for static mapping in the IPv4 network.

params.name: tcp
params.description: Applies static mapping to TCP protocol packets.

params.name: port
params.description: Port number of the IPv6 or IPv4 address. Valid values are from 1 to 65535.

params.name: udp
params.description: Applies static mapping to UDP protocol packets.

params.name: forced
params.description: (Optional) Removes the configuration even when the NAT64 translation exists for the configuration.

name: show ip nat translations
detail: Display active Network Address Translation ( NAT) translations.
syntax: show ip nat translations [ inside global-ip ] [ outside local-ip ] [icmp] [tcp] [udp] [verbose] [ vrf vrf-name ]

params.name: icmp
params.description: (Optional) Displays Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) entries.

params.name: inside global-ip
params.description: (Optional) Displays entries for only a specific inside global IP address.

params.name: outside local-ip
params.description: (Optional) Displays entries for only a specific outside local IP address.

params.name: tcp
params.description: (Optional) Displays TCP protocol entries.

params.name: udp
params.description: (Optional) Displays User Datagram Protocol (UDP) entries.

params.name: verbose
params.description: (Optional) Displays additional information for each translation table entry, including how long ago the entry was created and used.

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Displays VPN routing and forwarding (VRF) traffic-related information.

name: show ip nhrp nhs
detail: To display Next Hop Resolution Protocol (NHRP) next hop server (NHS) information, use the show ip nhrp nhscommand in user EXEC or privileged EXEC mode.
syntax: show ip nhrp nhs [interface] [detail] [redundancy [cluster number | preempted | running | waiting]]

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) Displays NHS information currently configured on the interface. See the table below for types, number ranges, and descriptions.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed NHS information.

params.name: redundancy
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about NHS redundancy stacks. cluster number (Optional) Displays redundancy cluster information.

params.name: preempted
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about NHS that failed to become active and is preempted.

params.name: running
params.description: (Optional) Displays NHSs that are currently in Responding or Expecting replies states.

params.name: waiting
params.description: (Optional) Displays NHSs awaiting to be scheduled.

usageSummary: The table below lists the valid types, number ranges, and descriptions for the optional interfaceargument.

name: show ip ports all
detail: To display all the open ports on a device, use the show ip ports all in user EXEC or privileged EXEC mode.
usageSummary: This command provides a list of all open TCP/IP ports on the system including the ports opened using Cisco networking stack.  To close open ports, you can use one of the following methods: • Use Access Control List (ACL).  • To close the UDP 2228 port, use the no l2 traceroute command.

name: show ip wccp
detail: Display the IPv4 Web Cache Communication Protocol (WCCP) global configuration and statistics.
syntax: show ip wccp [ all ] [capabilities] [summary] [ interfaces [ cef | counts | detail ] ] [ vrf vrf-name ] [ { web-cache service-number } [assignment] [clients] [counters] [detail] [service] [view] ]

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays statistics for all known services.

params.name: capabilities
params.description: (Optional) Displays WCCP platform capabilities information.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays a summary of WCCP services.

params.name: interfaces
params.description: (Optional) Displays WCCP redirect interfaces.

params.name: cef
params.description: (Optional) Displays Cisco Express Forwarding interface statistics, including the number of input, output, dynamic, static, and multicast services.

params.name: counts
params.description: (Optional) Displays WCCP interface count statistics, including the number of Cisco Express Forwarding and process-switched output and input packets redirected.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays WCCP interface configuration statistics, including the number of input, output, dynamic, static, and multicast services.

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) instance associated with a service group to display.

params.name: web-cache
params.description: (Optional) Displays statistics for the web cache service.

params.name: service-number
params.description: (Optional) Identification number of the web cache service group being controlled by the cache. The number can be from 0 to 254. For web caches using Cisco cache engines, the reverse proxy service is indicated by a value of 99.

params.name: assignment
params.description: (Optional) Displays service group assignment information.

params.name: clients
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed information about the clients of a service, including all per-client information. No per-service information is displayed.

params.name: counters
params.description: (Optional) Displays traffic counters.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed information about the clients of a service, including all per-client information. No per-service information is displayed. Assignment information is also displayed.

params.name: service
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed information about a service, including the service definition and all other per-service information.

params.name: view
params.description: (Optional) Displays other members of a particular service group, or all service groups, that have or have not been detected. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 649 IP Addressing Services show ip wccp

usageSummary: Use the clear ip wccp command to reset all WCCP counters.  Use the show ip wccp service-number detail command to display information about the WCCP client timeout interval and the redirect assignment timeout interval if those intervals are not set to their default value of 10 seconds.  Use the show ip wccp summary command to display the configured WCCP services and a summary of their current state.

name: show ipv6 access-list
detail: Display the contents of all the current IPv6 access lists.

params.name: access-list-name
params.description: (Optional) Name of the access list.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 access-list command provides output similar to the show ip access-list command, except that it is IPv6-specific.

name: show ipv6 destination-guard policy
detail: Display destination guard information.

params.name: policy-name
params.description: (Optional) Name of the destination guard policy.

usageSummary: If the policy-name argument is specified, only the specified policy information is displayed.  If the policy-name argument is not specified, information is displayed for all policies.

name: show ipv6 dhcp
detail: Display the Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) unique identifier (DUID) on a specified device.
usageSummary: The show ipv6 dhcp command uses the DUID based on the link-layer address for both client and server identifiers.  The device uses the MAC address from the lowest-numbered interface to form the DUID.  The network interface is assumed to be permanently attached to the device.

name: show ipv6 dhcp binding
detail: Display automatic client bindings from the Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) for IPv6 server binding table.

params.name: ipv6-address
params.description: (Optional) The address of a DHCP for IPv6 client.

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 dhcp binding command displays all automatic client bindings from the DHCP for IPv6 server binding table if the ipv6-address argument is not specified.  When the ipv6-address argument is specified, only the binding for the specified client is displayed.  If the vrf vrf-namekeyword and argument combination is specified, all bindings that belong to the specified VRF are displayed.

name: show ipv6 dhcp conflict
detail: Display address conflicts found by a Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol for IPv6 (DHCPv6) server when addresses are offered to the client.

params.name: ipv6-address
params.description: (Optional) The address of a DHCP for IPv6 client.

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

usageSummary: When you configure the DHCPv6 server to detect conflicts, it uses ping.  The client uses neighbor discovery to detect clients and reports to the server through a DECLINE message.  If an address conflict is detected, the address is removed from the pool, and the address is not assigned until the administrator removes the address from the conflict list.

name: show ipv6 dhcp database
detail: Display the Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) for IPv6 binding database agent information.

params.name: agent-URL
params.description: (Optional) A flash, NVRAM, FTP, TFTP, or remote copy protocol (RCP) uniform resource locator.

usageSummary: Each permanent storage to which the binding database is saved is called the database agent.  An agent can be configured using the ipv6 dhcp database command.  Supported database agents include FTP and TFTP servers, RCP, Flash file system, and NVRAM.

name: show ipv6 dhcp guard policy
detail: Display Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol for IPv6 (DHCPv6) guard information.

params.name: policy-name
params.description: (Optional) DHCPv6 guard policy name.

usageSummary: If the policy-name argument is specified, only the specified policy information is displayed.  If the policy-name argument is not specified, information is displayed for all policies.

name: show ipv6 dhcp interface
detail: Display Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) for IPv6 interface information.

params.name: type number
params.description: (Optional) Interface type and number. For more information, use the question mark (?) online help function.

usageSummary: If no interfaces are specified, all interfaces on which DHCP for IPv6 (client or server) is enabled are shown.  If an interface is specified, only information about the specified interface is displayed.

name: show ipv6 dhcp relay binding
detail: Display DHCPv6 Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA) and DHCPv6 Identity Association for Prefix Delegation (IAPD) bindings on a relay agent.
syntax: show ipv6 dhcp relay binding [vrf vrf-name]

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

usageSummary: If the vrf vrf-name keyword-argument pair is specified, all bindings belonging to the specified VRF are displayed.

name: show ipv6 eigrp events
detail: Display Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol (EIGRP) events logged for IPv6.
syntax: show ipv6 eigrp events [[errmsg | sia] [event-num-start event-num-end] | type]

params.name: errmsg
params.description: (Optional) Displays error messages being logged.

params.name: sia
params.description: (Optional) Displays Stuck In Active (SIA) messages.

params.name: event-num-start
params.description: (Optional) Starting number of the event range. The range is from 1 to 4294967295.

params.name: event-num-end
params.description: (Optional) Ending number of the event range. The range is from 1 to 4294967295.

params.name: type
params.description: (Optional) Displays event types being logged.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 eigrp events command is used to analyze a network failure by the Cisco support team and is not intended for general use.  This command provides internal state information about EIGRP and how it processes route notifications and changes.

name: show ipv6 eigrp interfaces
detail: Display information about interfaces configured for the Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol (EIGRP) in IPv6 topologies.

params.name: as-number
params.description: (Optional) Autonomous system number.

params.name: type
params.description: (Optional) Interface type. For more information, use the question mark (?) online help function.

params.name: number
params.description: (Optional) Interface number. For more information about the numbering syntax for your networking device, use the question mark (?) online help function.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed interface information.

usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 eigrp interfaces command to determine the interfaces on which EIGRP is active and to get information about EIGRP processes related to those interfaces.  The optional type number argument and the detail keyword can be entered in any order.  If an interface is specified, only that interface is displayed.

name: show ipv6 eigrp topology
detail: Display Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol (EIGRP) IPv6 topology table entries.
syntax: show ipv6 eigrp topology [as-number ipv6-address] [active | all-links | pending | summary | zero-successors]

params.name: as-number
params.description: (Optional) Autonomous system number.

params.name: ipv6-address
params.description: (Optional) IPv6 address.

params.name: active
params.description: (Optional) Displays only active entries in the EIGRP topology table.

params.name: all-links
params.description: (Optional) Displays all entries in the EIGRP topology table (including nonfeasible-successor sources).

params.name: pending
params.description: (Optional) Displays all entries in the EIGRP topology table that are either waiting for an update from a neighbor or waiting to reply to a neighbor.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays a summary of the EIGRP topology table.

params.name: zero-successors
params.description: (Optional) Displays the available routes that have zero successors.

usageSummary: If this command is used without any keywords or arguments, only routes that are feasible successors are displayed.  The show ipv6 eigrp topology command can be used to determine Diffusing Update Algorithm (DUAL) states and to debug possible DUAL problems.

name: show ipv6 eigrp traffic
detail: Display the number of Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol (EIGRP) for IPv6 packets sent and received.

params.name: as-number
params.description: (Optional) Autonomous system number.

usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 eigrp traffic command to provide information on packets received and sent.

name: show ipv6 general-prefix
detail: Display information on IPv6 general prefixes.
usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 general-prefix command to view information on IPv6 general prefixes.

name: show ipv6 interface
detail: Display the usability status of interfaces configured for IPv6.

params.name: brief
params.description: (Optional) Displays a brief summary of IPv6 status and configuration for each interface.

params.name: type
params.description: (Optional) The interface type about which to display information.

params.name: number
params.description: (Optional) The interface number about which to display information.

params.name: prefix
params.description: (Optional) Prefix generated from a local IPv6 prefix pool.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 interface command provides output similar to the show ip interface command, except that it is IPv6-specific.  Use the show ipv6 interface command to validate the IPv6 status of an interface and its configured addresses.  The show ipv6 interface command also displays the parameters that IPv6 is using for operation on this interface and any configured features.

name: show ipv6 mfib
detail: Display the forwarding entries and interfaces in the IPv6 Multicast Forwarding Information Base (MFIB).
syntax: show ipv6 mfib [vrf vrf-name] [all | linkscope | verbose group-address-name | ipv6-prefix/ prefix-length source-address-name | interface | status | summary] show ipv6 mfib [vrf vrf-name] [all | linkscope | verbose | interface | status | summary]

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name            (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays all forwarding entries and interfaces in the IPv6 MFIB.

params.name: linkscope
params.description: (Optional) Displays the link-local groups.

params.name: verbose
params.description: (Optional) Provides additional information, such as the MAC encapsulation header and platform-specific information.

params.name: ipv6-prefix
params.description: (Optional) The IPv6 network assigned to the interface. The default IPv6 prefix is 128. This argument must be in the form documented in RFC 2373 where the address is specified in hexadecimal using 16-bit values between colons.

params.name: / prefix-length
params.description: (Optional) The length of the IPv6 prefix. A decimal value that indicates how many of the high-order contiguous bits of the address comprise the prefix (the network portion of the address). A slash mark must precede the decimal value.

params.name: group-address-name
params.description: (Optional) IPv6 address or name of the multicast group.

params.name: source-address-name
params.description: (Optional) IPv6 address or name of the multicast group.

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) Interface settings and status.

params.name: status
params.description: (Optional) General settings and status.

usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 mfib command to display MFIB entries; and forwarding interfaces, and their traffic statistics.  This command can be enabled on virtual IP (VIP) if the router is operating in distributed mode.  A forwarding entry in the MFIB has flags that determine the default forwarding and signaling behavior to use for packets matching the entry.

name: show ipv6 mld groups
detail: Display the multicast groups that are directly connected to the router and that were learned through Multicast Listener Discovery (MLD).
syntax: show ipv6 mld [vrf vrf-name] groups [link-local] [group-namegroup-address] [interface-type interface-number] [detail | explicit]

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: link-local
params.description: (Optional) Displays the link-local groups.

params.name: group-name | group-address
params.description: (Optional) IPv6 address or name of the multicast group.

params.name: interface-type interface-number
params.description: (Optional) Interface type and number.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed information about individual sources.

params.name: explicit
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the hosts being explicitly tracked on each interface for each group.

usageSummary: If you omit all optional arguments, the show ipv6 mld groups command displays by group address and interface type and number all directly connected multicast groups, including link-local groups (where the link-local keyword is not available) used.

name: show ipv6 mld interface
detail: Display multicast-related information about an interface.
syntax: show ipv6 mld [vrf vrf-name] interface [type number]

params.name: type number
params.description: (Optional) Interface type and number.

usageSummary: If you omit the optional type and number arguments, the show ipv6 mld interface command displays information about all interfaces.

name: show ipv6 mld snooping
detail: Use the show ipv6 mld snooping command in EXEC mode to display IP version 6 (IPv6) Multicast Listener Discovery (MLD) snooping configuration of the switch or the VLAN.

params.name: vlan
params.description: vlan-id   (Optional) Specify a VLAN; the range is 1 to 1001 and 1006 to 4094.

usageSummary: Use this command to display MLD snooping configuration for the switch or for a specific VLAN.  VLAN numbers 1002 through 1005 are reserved for Token Ring and FDDI VLANs and cannot be used in MLD snooping.  To configure the dual IPv4 and IPv6 template, enter the sdm prefer dual-ipv4-and-ipv6 global configuration command and reload the switch.

name: show ipv6 mld ssm-map
detail: Display Source Specific Multicast (SSM) mapping information.

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name      (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: source-address
params.description: (Optional) Source address associated with an MLD membership for a group identified by the access list.

usageSummary: If the optional source-address argument is not used, all SSM mapping information is displayed.

name: show ipv6 mld traffic
detail: Display the Multicast Listener Discovery (MLD) traffic counters.

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name       (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 mld traffic command to check if the expected number of MLD protocol messages have been received and sent.

name: show ipv6 mrib client
detail: Display information about the clients of the Multicast Routing Information Base (MRIB).
syntax: show ipv6 mrib [vrf vrf-name] client [filter] [name {client-name | client-name : client-id}]

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name           (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: filter
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about MRIB flags that each client owns and that each client is interested in.

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) The name of a multicast routing protocol that acts as a client of MRIB, such as Multicast Listener Discovery (MLD) and Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM).

params.name: client-name : client-id
params.description: The name and ID of a multicast routing protocol that acts as a client of MRIB, such as MLD and PIM. The colon is required.

usageSummary: Use the filter keyword to display information about the MRIB flags each client owns and the flags in which each client is interested.

name: show ipv6 mrib route
detail: Display Multicast Routing Information Base (MRIB) route information.
syntax: show ipv6 mrib [vrf vrf-name] route [link-local | summary | [source-addresssource-name | *] [groupname-or-address [prefix-length]]]

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name                   (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: link-local
params.description: (Optional) Displays the link-local groups.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays the number of MRIB entries (including link-local groups) and interfaces present in the MRIB table.

params.name: source address-or-name
params.description: (Optional) IPv6 address or name of the source.
(Optional) Displays all MRIB route information.

params.name: groupname or-address
params.description: (Optional) IPv6 address or name of the multicast group.

params.name: prefix-length
params.description: (Optional) IPv6 prefix length.

usageSummary: All entries are created by various clients of the MRIB, such as Multicast Listener Discovery (MLD), Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM), and Multicast Forwarding Information Base (MFIB).  The flags on each entry or interface serve as a communication mechanism between various clients of the MRIB.  The entries reveal how PIM sends register messages for new sources and the action taken.

name: show ipv6 mroute
detail: Display the information in the PIM topology table in a format similar to the show ip mroute command.

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: link-local
params.description: (Optional) Displays the link-local groups.

params.name: group-name | group-address
params.description: (Optional) IPv6 address or name of the multicast group. source-address | source-name (Optional) IPv6 address or name of the source.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays a one-line, abbreviated summary of each entry in the IPv6 multicast routing table.

params.name: count
params.description: (Optional) Displays statistics from the Multicast Forwarding Information Base (MFIB) about the group and source, including number of packets, packets per second, average packet size, and bytes per second.

usageSummary: The IPv6 multicast implementation does not have a separate mroute table.  For this reason, the show ipv6 mroute command enables you to display the information in the PIM topology table in a format similar to the show ip mroute command.  If you omit all optional arguments and keywords, the show ipv6 mroute command displays all the entries in the PIM topology table (except link-local groups where the link-local keyword is available).

name: show ipv6 mtu
detail: Display maximum transmission unit (MTU) cache information for IPv6 interfaces.

params.name: vrf
params.description: (Optional) Displays an IPv6 Virtual Private Network (VPN) routing/forwarding instance (VRF).

params.name: vrfname
params.description: (Optional) Name of the IPv6 VRF.

usageSummary: The vrf keyword and vrfname argument allow you to view MTUs related to a specific VRF.

name: show ipv6 nd destination
detail: Display information about IPv6 host-mode destination cache entries.
syntax: show ipv6 nd destination[vrf vrf-name][interface-type interface-number]

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name        (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: interface- type
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the Interface type. interface- number (Optional) Specifies the Interface number.

usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 nd destination command to display information about IPv6 host-mode destination cache entries.  If the vrf vrf-name keyword and argument pair is used, then only information about the specified VRF is displayed.  If the interface-type and interface-number arguments are used, then only information about the specified interface is displayed.

name: show ipv6 nd on-link prefix
detail: Display information about on-link prefixes learned through router advertisements (RAs).
syntax: show ipv6 nd on-link prefix[vrf vrf-name][interface-type interface-number]

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name       (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: interface -type
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the Interface type. interface -number (Optional) Specifies the Interface number.

usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 nd on-link prefix command to display information about on-link prefixes learned through RAs.  Prefixes learned from an RA may be inspected using the show ipv6 nd on-link prefix command.  If the vrf vrf-name keyword and argument pair is used, then only information about the specified VRF is displayed.

name: show ipv6 nd routing-proxy
detail: Display the routing-proxy default configuration and all targets it is applied to.

name: show ipv6 neighbors
detail: Display IPv6 neighbor discovery (ND) cache information.

params.name: interface-type
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the type of the interface from which IPv6 neighbor information is to be displayed. interface-number (Optional) Specifies the number of the interface from which IPv6 neighbor information is to be displayed.

params.name: ipv6-address
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the IPv6 address of the neighbor. This argument must be in the form documented in RFC 2373 where the address is specified in hexadecimal using 16-bit values between colons.

params.name: ipv6-hostname
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the IPv6 hostname of the remote networking device.

params.name: statistics
params.description: (Optional) Displays ND cache statistics.

usageSummary: When the interface-type and interface-number arguments are not specified, cache information for all IPv6 neighbors is displayed.  Specifying the interface-type and interface-number arguments displays only cache information about the specified interface.  Specifying the statistics keyword displays ND cache statistics.

name: show ipv6 nhrp
detail: Display Next Hop Resolution Protocol (NHRP) mapping information.
syntax: show ipv6 nhrp [dynamic [ipv6-address] | incomplete | static] [address | interface] [brief | detail] [purge]

params.name: dynamic
params.description: (Optional) Displays dynamic (learned) IPv6-to-nonbroadcast multiaccess address (NBMA) mapping entries. Dynamic NHRP mapping entries are obtained from NHRP resolution/registration exchanges. See the table below for types, number ranges, and descriptions.

params.name: ipv6-address
params.description: (Optional) The IPv6 address of the cache entry.

params.name: incomplete
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about NHRP mapping entries for which the IPv6-to-NBMA is not resolved. See the table below for types, number ranges, and descriptions.

params.name: static
params.description: (Optional) Displays static IPv6-to-NBMA address mapping entries. Static NHRP mapping entries are configured using the ipv6 nhrp map command. See the table below for types, number ranges, and descriptions.

params.name: address
params.description: (Optional) NHRP mapping entry for specified protocol addresses.

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) NHRP mapping entry for the specified interface. See the table below for types, number ranges, and descriptions.

params.name: brief
params.description: (Optional) Displays a short output of the NHRP mapping.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed information about NHRP mapping.

params.name: purge
params.description: (Optional) Displays NHRP purge information.

usageSummary: The table below lists the valid types, number ranges, and descriptions for the optional interface argument.

name: show ipv6 ospf
detail: Display general information about Open Shortest Path First ( OSPF) routing processes.

params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) Internal identification. It is locally assigned and can be any positive integer. The number used here is the number assigned administratively when the OSPF routing process is enabled.

params.name: area-id
params.description: (Optional) Area ID. This argument displays information about a specified area only.

params.name: rate-limit
params.description: (Optional) Rate-limited link-state advertisements (LSAs). This keyword displays LSAs that are currently being rate limited, together with the remaining time to the next generation.

name: show ipv6 ospf border-routers
detail: Display the internal Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) routing table entries to an Area Border Router (ABR) and Autonomous System Boundary Router (ASBR).

params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) Internal identification. It is locally assigned and can be any positive integer. The number used here is the number assigned administratively when the OSPF routing process is enabled.

name: show ipv6 ospf event
detail: Display detailed information about IPv6 Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) events.
syntax: show ipv6 ospf [process-id] event [generic | interface | lsa | neighbor | reverse | rib | spf]

params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) Internal identification. It is locally assigned and can be any positive integer. The number used here is the number assigned administratively when the OSPF routing process is enabled.

params.name: generic
params.description: (Optional) Generic information regarding OSPF for IPv6 events.

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) Interface state change events, including old and new states.

params.name: lsa
params.description: (Optional) LSA arrival and LSA generation events.

params.name: neighbor
params.description: (Optional) Neighbor state change events, including old and new states.

params.name: reverse
params.description: (Optional) Keyword to allow the display of events in reverse-from the latest to the oldest or from oldest to the latest.

params.name: rib
params.description: (Optional) Routing Information Base (RIB) update, delete, and redistribution events.

params.name: spf
params.description: (Optional) Scheduling and SPF run events.

usageSummary: An OSPF event log is kept for every OSPF instance.  If you enter no keywords with the show ipv6 ospf event command, all information in the OSPF event log is displayed.  Use the keywords to filter specific information.

name: show ipv6 ospf graceful-restart
detail: Display Open Shortest Path First for IPv6 (OSPFv3) graceful restart information.
usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 ospf graceful-restart command to discover information about the OSPFv3 graceful restart feature.

name: show ipv6 ospf interface
detail: Display Open Shortest Path First (OSPF)-related interface information.

params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) Internal identification. It is locally assigned and can be any positive integer. The number used here is the number assigned administratively when the OSPF routing process is enabled.

params.name: area-id
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about a specified area only.

params.name: type number
params.description: (Optional) Interface type and number.

params.name: brief
params.description: (Optional) Displays brief overview information for OSPF interfaces, states, addresses and masks, and areas on the router.

name: show ipv6 ospf request-list
detail: Display a list of all link-state advertisements (LSAs) requested by a router.

params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) Internal identification. It is locally assigned and can be any positive integer. The number used here is the number assigned administratively when the Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) routing process is enabled.

params.name: area-id
params.description: (Optional) Displays information only about a specified area.

params.name: neighbor
params.description: (Optional) Displays the list of all LSAs requested by the router from this neighbor.

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) Displays the list of all LSAs requested by the router from this interface.

params.name: interface-neighbor
params.description: (Optional) Displays the list of all LSAs requested by the router on this interface, from this neighbor.

usageSummary: The information displayed by the show ipv6 ospf request-list command is useful in debugging OSPF routing operations.

name: show ipv6 ospf retransmission-list
detail: Display a list of all link-state advertisements (LSAs) waiting to be re-sent.

params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) Internal identification. It is locally assigned and can be any positive integer. The number used here is the number assigned administratively when the OSPF routing process is enabled.

params.name: area-id
params.description: (Optional) Displays information only about a specified area.

params.name: neighbor
params.description: (Optional) Displays the list of all LSAs waiting to be re-sent for this neighbor.

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) Displays the list of all LSAs waiting to be re-sent on this interface.

params.name: interface neighbor
params.description: (Optional) Displays the list of all LSAs waiting to be re-sent on this interface, from this neighbor.

usageSummary: The information displayed by the show ipv6 ospf retransmission-list command is useful in debugging Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) routing operations.

name: show ipv6 ospf statistics
detail: Display Open Shortest Path First for IPv6 (OSPFv6) shortest path first (SPF) calculation statistics.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays statistics separately for each OSPF area and includes additional, more detailed statistics.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 ospf statistics command provides important information about SPF calculations and the events that trigger them.  This information can be meaningful for both OSPF network maintenance and troubleshooting.  For example, entering the show ipv6 ospf statistics command is recommended as the first troubleshooting step for link-state advertisement (LSA) flapping.

name: show ipv6 ospf summary-prefix
detail: Display a list of all summary address redistribution information configured under an OSPF process.

params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) Internal identification. It is locally assigned and can be any positive integer. The number used here is the number assigned administratively when the OSPF routing process is enabled.

usageSummary: The process-id argument can be entered as a decimal number or as an IPv6 address format.

name: show ipv6 ospf timers rate-limit
detail: Display all of the link-state advertisements (LSAs) in the rate limit queue.
usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 ospf timers rate-limit command to discover when LSAs in the queue will be sent.

name: show ipv6 ospf traffic
detail: Display IPv6 Open Shortest Path First Version 3 (OSPFv3) traffic statistics.

params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) OSPF process ID for which you want traffic statistics (for example, queue statistics, statistics for each interface under the OSPF process, and per OSPF process statistics). interface-type interface-number (Optional) Type and number associated with a specific OSPF interface.

usageSummary: You can limit the displayed traffic statistics to those for a specific OSPF process by entering a value for the process-id argument, or you can limit output to traffic statistics for a specific interface associated with an OSPF process by entering values for the interface-type and interface-number arguments.  To reset counters and clear statistics, use the clear ipv6 ospf traffic command.

name: show ipv6 ospf virtual-links
detail: Display parameters and the current state of Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) virtual links.
usageSummary: The information displayed by the show ipv6 ospf virtual-links command is useful in debugging OSPF routing operations.

name: show ipv6 pim anycast-RP
detail: Verify IPv6 PIM anycast RP operation.

params.name: rp-address
params.description: RP address to be verified.

name: show ipv6 pim bsr
detail: Display information related to Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) bootstrap router (BSR) protocol processing.
syntax: show ipv6 pim [vrf vrf-name] bsr {election | rp-cache | candidate-rp}

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: election
params.description: Displays BSR state, BSR election, and bootstrap message (BSM)-related timers.

params.name: rp-cache
params.description: Displays candidate rendezvous point (C-RP) cache learned from unicast C-RP announcements on the elected BSR.

params.name: candidate-rp
params.description: Displays C-RP state on devices that are configured as C-RPs.

usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 pim bsr command to display details of the BSR election-state machine, C-RP advertisement state machine, and the C-RP cache.  Information on the C-RP cache is displayed only on the elected BSR device, and information on the C-RP state machine is displayed only on a device configured as a C-RP.

name: show ipv6 pim df
detail: Display the designated forwarder (DF)-election state of each interface for each rendezvous point (RP).
syntax: show ipv6 pim [vrf vrf-name] df [interface-type interface-number] [rp-address]

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name                         (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: interface-type interface-number
params.description: (Optional) Interface type and number. For more information, use the question mark (?) online help function.

params.name: rp-address
params.description: (Optional) RP IPv6 address.

usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 pim df command to display the state of the DF election for each RP on each Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM)-enabled interface if the bidirectional multicast traffic is not flowing as expected.

name: show ipv6 pim group-map
detail: Display an IPv6 Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) group mapping table.
syntax: {show ipv6 pim [vrf vrf-name] group-map [group-namegroup-address] | [group-rangegroup-mask] [info-source {bsr | default | embedded-rp | static}]}

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration. group-name | group-address (Optional) IPv6 address or name of the multicast group.

params.name: group-range | group-mask
params.description: (Optional) Group range list. Includes group ranges with the same prefix or mask length.

params.name: info-source
params.description: (Optional) Displays all mappings learned from a specific source, such as the bootstrap router (BSR) or static configuration.

params.name: bsr
params.description: Displays ranges learned through the BSR.

params.name: default
params.description: Displays ranges enabled by default.

params.name: embedded-rp
params.description: Displays group ranges learned through the embedded rendezvous point (RP).

params.name: static
params.description: Displays ranges enabled by static configuration.

usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 pim group-map command to find all group mappings installed by a given source of information, such as BSR or static configuration.  You can also use this command to find which group mapping a router at a specified IPv6 group address is using by specifying a group address, or to find an exact group mapping entry by specifying a group range and mask length.

name: show ipv6 pim interface
detail: Display information about interfaces configured for Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM).
syntax: show ipv6 pim [vrf vrf-name] interface [state-on] [state-off] [type number]

params.name: state-on
params.description: (Optional) Displays interfaces with PIM enabled.

params.name: state-off
params.description: (Optional) Displays interfaces with PIM disabled.

params.name: type number
params.description: (Optional) Interface type and number.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 pim interface command is used to check if PIM is enabled on an interface, the number of neighbors, and the designated router (DR) on the interface.

name: show ipv6 pim join-prune statistic
detail: Display the average join-prune aggregation for the most recently aggregated 1000, 10,000, and 50,000 packets for each interface.
syntax: show ipv6 pim [vrf vrf-name] join-prune statistic [interface-type]

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name      (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: interface-type
params.description: (Optional) Interface type. For more information, use the question mark (?) online help function.

usageSummary: When Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) sends multiple joins and prunes simultaneously, it aggregates them into a single packet.  The show ipv6 pim join-prune statistic command displays the average number of joins and prunes that were aggregated into a single packet over the last 1000 PIM join-prune packets, over the last 10,000 PIM join-prune packets, and over the last 50,000 PIM join-prune packets.

name: show ipv6 pim limit
detail: Display Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) interface limit.

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name   (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) Specific interface for which limit information is provided.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 pim limit command checks interface statistics for limits.  If the optional interface argument is enabled, only information for the specified interface is shown.

name: show ipv6 pim neighbor
detail: Display the Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) neighbors discovered by the Cisco software.
syntax: show ipv6 pim [vrf vrf-name ]neighbor [detail ][interface-type interface-number | count]

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays the additional addresses of the neighbors learned, if any, through the routable address hello option. interface-type interface-number (Optional) Interface type and number.

params.name: count
params.description: (Optional) Displays neighbor counts on each interface.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 pim neighbor command displays which routers on the LAN are configured for PIM.

name: show ipv6 pim range-list
detail: Display information about IPv6 multicast range lists.

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: config
params.description: (Optional) The client. Displays the range lists configured on the router. rp-address | rp-name (Optional) The address of a Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) rendezvous point (RP).

usageSummary: The show ipv6 pim range-list command displays IPv6 multicast range lists on a per-client and per-mode basis.  A client is the entity from which the specified range list was learned.  The clients can be config, and the modes can be Source Specific Multicast (SSM) or sparse mode (SM).

name: show ipv6 pim topology
detail: Display Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) topology table information for a specific group or all groups.
syntax: show ipv6 pim [vrf vrf-name] topology [group-name | group-address [source-addresssource-name] | link-local]route-count [detail]

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name                     (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: group-name | group-address
params.description: (Optional) IPv6 address or name of the multicast group. source-address | source-name (Optional) IPv6 address or name of the source.

params.name: link-local
params.description: (Optional) Displays the link-local groups.

params.name: route-count
params.description: (Optional) Displays the number of routes in PIM topology table.

usageSummary: This command shows the PIM topology table for a given group--(*, G), (S, G), and (S, G) Rendezvous Point Tree (RPT)-- as internally stored in a PIM topology table.  The PIM topology table may have various entries for a given group, each with its own interface list.  The resulting forwarding state is maintained in the Multicast Routing Information Base (MRIB) table, which shows which interface the data packet should be accepted on and which interfaces the data packet should be forwarded to for a given (S, G) entry.

name: show ipv6 pim traffic
detail: Display the Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) traffic counters.

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 pim traffic command to check if the expected number of PIM protocol messages have been received and sent.

name: show ipv6 pim tunnel
detail: Display information about the Protocol Independent Multicast (PIM) register encapsulation and de-encapsulation tunnels on an interface.
syntax: show ipv6 pim [vrf vrf-name] tunnel [interface-type interface-number]

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: interface-type interface-number
params.description: (Optional) Tunnel interface type and number.

usageSummary: If you use the show ipv6 pim tunnel command without the optional interface keyword, information about the PIM register encapsulation and de-encapsulation tunnel interfaces is displayed.  The PIM encapsulation tunnel is the register tunnel.  An encapsulation tunnel is created for every known rendezvous point (RP) on each router.

name: show ipv6 policy
detail: Display the IPv6 policy-based routing (PBR) configuration.
usageSummary: IPv6 policy matches will be counted on route maps, as is done in IPv4.  Therefore, IPv6 policy matches can also be displayed on the show route-map command.

name: show ipv6 prefix-list
detail: Display information about an IPv6 prefix list or IPv6 prefix list entries.
syntax: show ipv6 prefix-list [detail | summary] [list-name] show ipv6 prefix-list list-name ipv6-prefix/prefix-length [longer | first-match] show ipv6 prefix-list list-name seq seq-num

params.name: list-name
params.description: (Optional) The name of a specific IPv6 prefix list.

params.name: ipv6-prefix
params.description: All prefix list entries for the specified IPv6 network. This argument must be in the form documented in RFC 2373 where the address is specified in hexadecimal using 16-bit values between colons.

params.name: / prefix-length
params.description: The length of the IPv6 prefix. A decimal value that indicates how many of the high-order contiguous bits of the address comprise the prefix (the network portion of the address). A slash mark must precede the decimal value.

params.name: longer
params.description: (Optional) Displays all entries of an IPv6 prefix list that are more specific than the given ipv6-prefix / prefix-lengthvalues.

params.name: first-match
params.description: (Optional) Displays the entry of an IPv6 prefix list that matches the given ipv6-prefix / prefix-lengthvalues.

params.name: seq seq-num
params.description: The sequence number of the IPv6 prefix list entry.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 prefix-list command provides output similar to the show ip prefix-list command, except that it is IPv6-specific.

name: show ipv6 protocols
detail: Display the parameters and the current state of the active IPv6 routing protocol processes.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays the configured routing protocol process names.

usageSummary: The information displayed by the show ipv6 protocols command is useful in debugging routing operations.

name: show ipv6 rip
detail: Display information about current IPv6 Routing Information Protocol (RIP) processes.
syntax: show ipv6 rip [name] [vrf vrf-name][database | next-hops] show ipv6 rip [name] [database | next-hops]

params.name: name
params.description: (Optional) Name of the RIP process. If the name is not entered, details of all configured RIP processes are displayed.

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the specified Virtual Routing and Forwarding (VRF) instance.

params.name: database
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about entries in the specified RIP IPv6 routing table.

params.name: next-hops
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the next hop addresses for the specified RIP IPv6 process. If no RIP process name is specified, the next-hop addresses for all RIP IPv6 processes are displayed.

name: show ipv6 route
detail: Display contents of the IPv6 routing table.
syntax: show ipv6 route [ipv6-address | ipv6-prefix/prefix-length [longer-prefixes] | [protocol] | [repair] | [updated [boot-up] [day month] [time]] | interface type number | nd | nsf | table table-id | watch]

params.name: ipv6-address
params.description: (Optional) Displays routing information for a specific IPv6 address.

params.name: ipv6-prefix
params.description: (Optional) Displays routing information for a specific IPv6 network.

params.name: /prefix-length
params.description: (Optional) The length of the IPv6 prefix. A decimal value that indicates how many of the high-order contiguous bits of the address comprise the prefix (the network portion of the address). A slash mark must precede the decimal value. longer-prefixes (Optional) Displays output for longer prefix entries.

params.name: protocol
params.description: (Optional) The name of a routing protocol or the keyword connected, local, mobile, or static. If you specify a routing protocol, use one of the following keywords: bgp, isis, eigrp, ospf, or rip.

params.name: repair
params.description: (Optional) Displays routes with repair paths.

params.name: updated
params.description: (Optional) Displays routes with time stamps.

params.name: boot-up
params.description: (Optional) Displays routing information since bootup.

params.name: day month
params.description: (Optional) Displays routes since the specified day and month.

params.name: time
params.description: (Optional) Displays routes since the specified time, in hh:mm format.

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the interface.

params.name: type
params.description: (Optional) Interface type.

params.name: number
params.description: (Optional) Interface number.

params.name: nd
params.description: (Optional) Displays only routes from the IPv6 Routing Information Base (RIB) that are owned by Neighbor Discovery (ND).

params.name: nsf
params.description: (Optional) Displays routes in the nonstop forwarding (NSF) state.

params.name: repair
params.description: (Optional)

params.name: table table-id
params.description: (Optional) Displays IPv6 RIB table information for the specified table ID. The table ID must be in hexadecimal format. The range is from 0 to 0-0xFFFFFFFF.

params.name: watch
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about route watchers.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 route command provides output similar to the show ip route command, except that the information is IPv6-specific.  When the ipv6-address or ipv6-prefix/prefix-length argument is specified, the longest match lookup is performed from the routing table, and only route information for that address or network is displayed.  When a routing protocol is specified, only routes for that protocol are displayed.

name: show ipv6 routers
detail: Display IPv6 router advertisement (RA) information received from on-link devices.

params.name: interface -type
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the Interface type. interface -number (Optional) Specifies the Interface number.

params.name: conflicts
params.description: (Optional) Displays RAs that differ from the RAs configured for a specified interface.

params.name: vrf
params.description: vrf-name      (Optional) Specifies a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) configuration.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Provides detail about the eligibility of the neighbor for election as the default device.

usageSummary: Devices that advertise parameters that differ from the RA parameters configured for the interface on which the RAs are received are marked as conflicting.

name: show ipv6 rpf
detail: Check Reverse Path Forwarding (RPF) information for a given unicast host address and prefix.
syntax: show ipv6 rpf {source-vrf [access-list] | vrf receiver-vrf {source-vrf [access-list] | select}}

params.name: vrf
params.description: Displays information about the VRF instance.

params.name: select
params.description: Displays group-to-VRF mapping information.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 rpf command displays information about how IPv6 multicast routing performs Reverse Path Forwarding (RPF).  Because the router can find RPF information from multiple routing tables (for example, unicast Routing Information Base [RIB], multiprotocol Border Gateway Protocol [BGP] routing table, or static mroutes), the show ipv6 rpf command to display the source from which the information is retrieved.

name: show ipv6 source-guard policy
detail: Display the IPv6 source-guard policy configuration.

params.name: source-guard-policy
params.description: User-defined name of the snooping policy. The policy name can be a symbolic string (such as Engineering) or an integer (such as 0).

usageSummary: The show ipv6 source-guard policy command displays the IPv6 source-guard policy configuration, as well as all the interfaces on which the policy is applied.  The command also displays IPv6 prefix guard information if the IPv6 prefix guard feature is enabled on the device.

name: show ipv6 spd
detail: Display the IPv6 Selective Packet Discard (SPD) configuration.
usageSummary: Use the show ipv6 spd command to display the SPD configuration, which may provide useful troubleshooting information.

name: show ipv6 static
detail: Display the current contents of the IPv6 routing table.
syntax: show ipv6 static [ipv6-address | ipv6-prefix/prefix-length] [interface type number | recursive] [detail]

params.name: ipv6-prefix
params.description: (Optional) Provides routing information for a specific IPv6 network. This argument must be in the form documented in RFC 2373 where the address is specified in hexadecimal using 16-bit values between colons. /prefix-length (Optional) The length of the IPv6 prefix. A decimal value that indicates how many of the high-order contiguous bits of the address comprise the prefix (the network portion of the address). A slash mark must precede the decimal value.

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) Name of an interface.

params.name: type
params.description: (Optional, but required if the interface keyword is used) Interface type. For a list of supported interface types, use the question mark (?) online help function.

params.name: number
params.description: (Optional, but required if the interface keyword is used) Interface number. For specific numbering syntax for supported interface types, use the question mark (?) online help function.

params.name: recursive
params.description: (Optional) Allows the display of recursive static routes only.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the following additional information: • For valid recursive routes, the output path set and maximum resolution depth. • For invalid recursive routes, the reason why the route is not valid. • For invalid direct or fully specified routes, the reason why the route is not valid.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 static command provides output similar to the show ip route command, except that it is IPv6-specific.  Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 18.

name: show ipv6 traffic
detail: Display statistics about IPv6 traffic.

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) All interfaces. IPv6 forwarding statistics for all interfaces on which IPv6 forwarding statistics are being kept will be displayed.

params.name: interface type number
params.description: (Optional) Specified interface. Interface statistics that have occurred since the statistics were last cleared on the specific interface are displayed.

usageSummary: The show ipv6 traffic command provides output similar to the show ip traffic command, except that it is IPv6-specific.

name: show key chain
detail: Display the keychain.

params.name: name-of-chain
params.description: (Optional) Name of the key chain to display, as named in the key chain command.

modes: Examples The following is sample output from the show key chain command: show key chain Device# show key chain

Key-chain AuthenticationGLBP: key 1 -- text "Thisisasecretkey" accept lifetime (always valid) - (always valid) [valid now] send lifetime (always valid) - (always valid) [valid now] Key-chain glbp2: key 100 -- text "abc123" accept lifetime (always valid) - (always valid) [valid now] send lifetime (always valid) - (always valid) [valid now]
key-string Specifies the authentication string for a key.
send-lifetime Sets the time period during which an authentication key on a key chain is valid to be sent.
Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.18.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 813 IP Addressing Services

name: show nat64 translations v4
detail: Display Network Address Translation 64 (NAT64) translations based on an IPv4 address.
syntax: show nat64 translation v4 {original ipv4-address | translated ipv6-address} total | verbose

params.name: original
params.description: Displays translations for the original IPv4 address.

params.name: ipv4-address
params.description: IPv4-address.

params.name: translated
params.description: Displays translations for the translated address.

params.name: ipv6-address
params.description: IPv6 network number to include in router advertisements. This argument must be in the form documented in RFC 2373 where the address is specified in hexadecimal using 16-bit values between colons.

params.name: total
params.description: (Optional) Displays the total NAT64 translation count.

params.name: verbose
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed NAT64 translation information.

name: show platform nat translations
detail: Display information about the static and dynamic NAT translations.
syntax: show platform nat translations { switch-number | active | standby } [ statistics ]

params.name: active
params.description: Selects the active instance of the switch.

params.name: standby
params.description: Selects the standby instance of the switch.

params.name: statistics
params.description: Shows the platform NAT statistics counters.

name: show track
detail: Display information about objects that are tracked by the tracking process.
syntax: show track [object-number [brief] | application [brief] | interface [brief] | ip[route [brief] | [sla [brief]] | ipv6 [route [brief]] | list [route [brief]] | resolution [ip | ipv6] | stub-object [brief] | summary | timers]

params.name: object-number
params.description: (Optional) Object number that represents the object to be tracked. The range is from 1 to 1000.

params.name: brief
params.description: (Optional) Displays a single line of information related to the preceding argument or keyword.

params.name: application
params.description: (Optional) Displays tracked application objects.

params.name: interface
params.description: (Optional) Displays tracked interface objects.

params.name: ip route
params.description: (Optional) Displays tracked IP route objects.

params.name: ip sla
params.description: (Optional) Displays tracked IP SLA objects.

params.name: ipv6 route
params.description: (Optional) Displays tracked IPv6 route objects.

params.name: list
params.description: (Optional) Displays the list of boolean objects.

params.name: resolution
params.description: (Optional) Displays resolution of tracked parameters.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays the summary of the specified object.

params.name: timers
params.description: (Optional) Displays polling interval timers.

usageSummary: Use this command to display information about objects that are tracked by the tracking process.  When no arguments or keywords are specified, information for all objects is displayed.  A maximum of 1000 objects can be tracked.

name: track
detail: Configure an interface to be tracked where the Gateway Load Balancing Protocol (GLBP) weighting changes based on the state of the interface.
syntax: track object-number interface type number {line-protocol | ip routing | ipv6 routing}
noForm: no track object-number interface type number {line-protocol | ip routing | ipv6 routing}

params.name: object-number
params.description: Object number in the range from 1 to 1000 representing the interface to be tracked. interface type number Interface type and number to be tracked.

params.name: line-protocol
params.description: Tracks whether the interface is up.

params.name: ip routing
params.description: Tracks whether IP routing is enabled, an IP address is configured on the interface, and the interface state is up, before reporting to GLBP that the interface is up.

params.name: ipv6 routing
params.description: Tracks whether IPv6 routing is enabled, an IP address is configured on the interface, and the interface state is up, before reporting to GLBP that the interface is up.

usageSummary: Use the track command in conjunction with the glbp weighting and glbp weighting track commands to configure parameters for an interface to be tracked.  If a tracked interface on a GLBP device goes down, the weighting for that device is reduced.  If the weighting falls below a specified minimum, the device will lose its ability to act as an active GLBP virtual forwarder.

name: vrrp
detail: Create a Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol version 3 (VRRPv3) group and enter VRRPv3 group configuration mode.

params.name: group-id
params.description: Virtual router group number. The range is from 1 to 255.

params.name: address-family
params.description: Specifies the address-family for this VRRP group.

params.name: ipv4
params.description: (Optional) Specifies IPv4 address.

params.name: ipv6
params.description: (Optional) Specifies IPv6 address.

name: vrrp description
detail: Assign a description to the Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol (VRRP) group.

params.name: text
params.description: Text (up to 80 characters) that describes the purpose or use of the group.

name: vrrp preempt
detail: Configure the device to take over as primary virtual router for a Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol (VRRP) group if it has higher priority than the current primary virtual router.
usageSummary: By default, the device being configured with this command will take over as primary virtual router for the group if it has a higher priority than the current primary virtual router.  You can configure a delay, which will cause the VRRP device to wait the specified number of seconds before issuing an advertisement claiming primary ownership.

name: vrrp priority
detail: Set the priority level of the device within a Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol (VRRP) group.

params.name: level
params.description: Priority of the device within the VRRP group. The range is from 1 to 254. The default is 100.

usageSummary: Use this command to control which device becomes the primary virtual router.

name: vrrp timers advertise
detail: Configure the interval between successive advertisements by the primary virtual router in a Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol (VRRP) group.

params.name: group
params.description: Virtual router group number. The group number range is from 1 to 255.

params.name: msec
params.description: (Optional) Changes the unit of the advertisement time from seconds to milliseconds. Without this keyword, the advertisement interval is in seconds.

params.name: interval
params.description: Time interval between successive advertisements by the primary virtual router. The unit of the interval is in seconds, unless the msec keyword is specified. The default is 1 second. The valid range is 1 to 255 seconds. When the msec keyword is specified, the valid range is 50 to 999 milliseconds.

usageSummary: The advertisements being sent by the primary virtual router communicate the state and priority of the current primary virtual router.  The vrrp timers advertise command configures the time between successive advertisement packets and the time before other routers declare the primary router to be down.  Routers or access servers on which timer values are not configured can learn timer values from the primary router.

name: vrrs leader
detail: Specify a leader’s name to be registered with Virtual Router Redundancy Service (VRRS).

params.name: vrrs-leader-name
params.description: Name of VRRS Tag to lead.
